articles.title: U.S. strikes Iran again. And, DOJ charges Google staffer over Polymarket trades
articles.summary: The U.S. says it has struck Iran again as peace talks continue to end the conflict. And, the federal government has charged a Google staffer for allegedly using insider information in Polymarket trades.
articles.titleZh: 美国再次打击伊朗。而且，司法部就 Polymarket 交易向谷歌员工提出指控
articles.summaryZh: 美国表示，随着和平谈判继续结束冲突，美国再次袭击了伊朗。而且，联邦政府还指控一名谷歌员工涉嫌在 Polymarket 交易中使用内幕信息。

contentParagraphs.en: Good morning. You're reading the Up First newsletter. Subscribe here to get it delivered to your inbox, and listen to the Up First podcast for all the news you need to start your day.
contentParagraphs.zh: 早上好。您正在阅读 Up First 时事通讯。在此订阅，将其发送到您的收件箱，并收听 Up First 播客，了解开始新的一天所需的所有新闻。

contentParagraphs.en: Today's top stories
contentParagraphs.zh: 今日头条新闻

contentParagraphs.en: U.S. forces shot down Iranian drones near the Strait of Hormuz and struck an area along Iran's coast overnight, according to a U.S. official who spoke to NPR. In retaliation for the attack on Bandar Abbas, Iran's Revolutionary Guard says this morning it targeted the American base that launched the attack. U.S. and Iran peace talks continue.
contentParagraphs.zh: 据一位接受美国国家公共广播电台采访的美国官员称，美军连夜在霍尔木兹海峡附近击落了伊朗无人机，并袭击了伊朗沿海地区。为了报复对阿巴斯港的袭击，伊朗革命卫队今天早上表示，他们的目标是发动袭击的美国基地。美国和伊朗的和平谈判仍在继续。

contentParagraphs.en: President Donald Trump (2nd-L) speaks as (L-R) Secretary of State Marco Rubio, Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth, Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick and Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy listen during a Cabinet meeting in the Cabinet Room of the White House on May 27, 2026, in Washington, D.C. Trump meets with his Cabinet days after saying a peace deal with Iran was "largely negotiated" amid expectations around the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
contentParagraphs.zh: 唐纳德·特朗普总统（左二）在 2026 年 5 月 27 日于华盛顿特区的白宫内阁会议室举行的内阁会议上讲话，（从左到右）国务卿马可·卢比奥、国防部长皮特·赫格塞斯、商务部长霍华德·卢特尼克和交通部长肖恩·达菲在听。几天后，特朗普表示与伊朗的和平协议“基本上已通过谈判”，在霍尔木兹海峡重新开放的预期下与伊朗达成了和平协议。

contentParagraphs.en: Win McNamee/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 赢得麦克纳米/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Win McNamee/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 赢得麦克纳米/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 It is difficult to determine if a deal is imminent, but all signs suggest that President Trump is pursuing a diplomatic solution with Iran instead of more war, NPR's Aya Batrawy tells Up First. The president has just shared a draft of the Iran deal with Israel's prime minister and several regional leaders, according to a person familiar with the matter who is not authorized to speak publicly. The interim deal would extend the ceasefire and reopen the Strait, but it would not include details on Iran's nuclear program. Iran has said there would be no deal with the U.S. without the war in Lebanon also coming to an end. Israel, a key U.S. ally, has expressed that it doesn't favor making a deal with Iran and wants to continue its war in Lebanon. Israel has issued evacuation warnings for Tyre, one of Lebanon's largest cities, deepening the conflict that has already displaced more than a million people in the country.
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的 Aya Batrawy 告诉 Up First，很难确定是否即将达成协议，但所有迹象都表明特朗普总统正在寻求与伊朗的外交解决方案，而不是更多的战争。据一位未获授权公开发言的知情人士透露，总统刚刚与以色列总理和几位地区领导人分享了伊朗协议草案。临时协议将延长停火并重新开放海峡，但不包括伊朗核计划的细节。伊朗表示，如果黎巴嫩战争不结束，就不会与美国达成协议。美国的重要盟友以色列已表示不赞成与伊朗达成协议，并希望继续在黎巴嫩进行战争。以色列向黎巴嫩最大城市之一提尔发出疏散警告，加剧了已经导致该国超过一百万人流离失所的冲突。

contentParagraphs.en: The director-general of the World Health Organization is expected to arrive in the Democratic Republic of Congo today, as the country struggles to contain an Ebola outbreak. Officials say there are more than 1,000 suspected cases and over 200 suspected deaths in the country. Congo's neighboring countries, Rwanda and Uganda, have closed their borders.
contentParagraphs.zh: 世界卫生组织总干事预计将于今天抵达刚果民主共和国，该国正在努力遏制埃博拉疫情。官员称，该国已有1000多例疑似病例，200多例疑似死亡。刚果的邻国卢旺达和乌干达已关闭边界。

contentParagraphs.en: 🎧 WHO's director-general has described eastern Congo as the site of a "catastrophic collision of disease and conflict," and reporter Emmet Livingstone says catastrophic sums up the situation. The region is suffering from militia violence and extreme poverty. The outbreak's epicenter is a remote gold-mining town called Mongbwalu, where health workers and NGOs face significant challenges. Some residents don't believe Ebola exists, and there have been violent attacks on hospitals and isolation tents. There are also certain areas of the town that have now become no-go zones. Livingstone spoke with Dr. Esther Sterk, a tropical disease specialist for Doctors Without Borders in Mongbwalu, who told him that hospitals and NGOs in the area are struggling to keep up with the scale of the outbreak.
contentParagraphs.zh: 🎧 世界卫生组织总干事将刚果东部描述为“疾病与冲突灾难性碰撞”的地点，记者埃米特·利文斯通（Emmet Livingstone）表示，灾难性的情况概括了这一情况。该地区正遭受民兵暴力和极端贫困之苦。此次疫情的震中是一个名为蒙布瓦鲁的偏远金矿小镇，那里的卫生工作者和非政府组织面临着严峻的挑战。一些居民不相信埃博拉病毒的存在，医院和隔离帐篷也发生过暴力袭击事件。该镇的某些区域现已成为禁区。利文斯通采访了蒙布瓦鲁无国界医生组织的热带病专家埃丝特·斯特克博士，她告诉他，该地区的医院和非政府组织正在努力应对疫情的规模。

contentParagraphs.en: More people in the United States are experiencing hunger now than during the COVID-19 pandemic six years ago, according to a survey released yesterday by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. The New York Fed periodically surveys Americans about their food security, inquiring about whether they skip meals, depend on food donations or receive federal assistance for groceries. Replies from the most recent survey, conducted in February, show 10% of families nationwide reported missing meals due to a lack of food, and nearly 16% relied on food donations. For families earning less than $50,000 a year, food insecurity rates were nearly double, with almost 20% having to skip meals or go without food.
contentParagraphs.zh: 纽约联邦储备银行昨天发布的一项调查显示，现在美国遭受饥饿的人数比六年前的 COVID-19 大流行期间还要多。纽约联储定期对美国人的食品安全情况进行调查，询问他们是否不吃饭、依赖食品捐赠或接受联邦食品杂货援助。 2 月份进行的最新调查的答复显示，全国有 10% 的家庭因缺乏食物而缺餐，近 16% 的家庭依赖食物捐赠。对于年收入低于 50,000 美元的家庭，粮食不安全率几乎翻倍，近 20% 的家庭不得不不吃饭或不吃饭。

contentParagraphs.en: A Google software engineer has been charged with leveraging confidential company information to make $1.2 million on the prediction market site Polymarket. This marks the second known case of the federal government filing criminal charges against someone for allegedly using insider information on a prediction market site. Michele Spagnuolo, an Italian citizen residing in Switzerland, was arrested yesterday and is facing charges of commodities fraud, wire fraud, money laundering and other counts for allegedly placing bets on search trends based on internal Google data that tracked user searches. Operating under the username AlphaRaccoon, the 36-year-old allegedly placed a number of bets on Google's most-searched person for 2025 and knew the outcomes of these wagers before the trading public did, according to prosecutors.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一名谷歌软件工程师被指控利用公司机密信息在预测市场网站 Polymarket 上赚取 120 万美元。这是联邦政府对涉嫌在预测市场网站上使用内幕信息的人提出刑事指控的第二起已知案件。居住在瑞士的意大利公民米歇尔·斯帕纽洛 (Michele Spagnuolo) 昨天被捕，因涉嫌根据跟踪用户搜索的谷歌内部数据对搜索趋势进行押注，面临商品欺诈、电信欺诈、洗钱和其他罪名的指控。检察官称，这名 36 岁的男子以 AlphaRaccoon 为用户名，据称对 2025 年谷歌搜索次数最多的人物进行了多次押注，并在交易公众之前知道了这些押注的结果。

contentParagraphs.en: Church and State
contentParagraphs.zh: 教会与国家

contentParagraphs.en: "The Prayer at Valley Forge", a 1975 painting by Arnold Friberg done for America's bicentennial celebration in 1976.
contentParagraphs.zh: 《福吉谷的祈祷》是阿诺德·弗里伯格 1975 年为 1976 年美国二百周年庆典创作的画作。

contentParagraphs.en: First Freedom Art Company
contentParagraphs.zh: 第一自由艺术公司

contentParagraphs.en: First Freedom Art Company
contentParagraphs.zh: 第一自由艺术公司

contentParagraphs.en: This week at NPR, we're going to explore the increased intermixing of God and government in a new series we're calling Church and State. It's an idea that has been gaining momentum through the first and second Trump administrations, not only within U.S. politics but also throughout American culture, in songs and paintings, schoolbooks and worship events infused with faith and politics.
contentParagraphs.zh: 本周，在 NPR，我们将在我们称之为“教会与国家”的新系列中探讨上帝与政府的日益混合。这一理念在特朗普第一届和第二届政府中不断增强，不仅在美国政界，而且在整个美国文化中，在充满信仰和政治的歌曲、绘画、教科书和礼拜活动中。

contentParagraphs.en: The Trump administration is using a painting that depicts George Washington praying to argue that the Founding Fathers were devout Christians. Historians question whether the moment in the painting actually occurred. The image has appeared in social media posts for the Defense Department, the Department of Labor, the Department of Homeland Security and more. It is even for sale on a federal government website that celebrates America's 250th anniversary. Many conservative evangelicals have adopted the 1975 Arnold Friberg painting, "The Prayer at Valley Forge," as a rebuke to mainstream historians. Nicholas Shrum, a doctoral student researching Latter-day Saint identity in Am
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普政府正在使用一幅描绘乔治·华盛顿祈祷的画作来证明开国元勋是虔诚的基督徒。历史学家质疑画中的那一刻是否真实发生过。该图像出现在国防部、劳工部、国土安全部等机构的社交媒体帖子中。它甚至在庆祝美国成立 250 周年的联邦政府网站上出售。许多保守派福音派人士采用了阿诺德·弗里伯格 1975 年的画作《福吉谷的祈祷》，作为对主流历史学家的谴责。尼古拉斯·施鲁姆 (Nicholas Shrum)，一名博士生，研究现代圣徒身份

articles.title: A federal judge in D.C. declines to block Trump's executive order on voting by mail
articles.summary: A federal judge in Washington, D.C., has declined to temporarily block President Trump's executive order that calls for restricting mail-in voting. Another judge may rule on the order soon.
articles.titleZh: 华盛顿特区的一名联邦法官拒绝阻止特朗普关于邮寄投票的行政命令
articles.summaryZh: 华盛顿特区的一名联邦法官拒绝暂时阻止特朗普总统要求限制邮寄投票的行政命令。另一位法官可能很快就会对该命令做出裁决。

contentParagraphs.en: President Trump holds his signed executive order that calls for restricting voting by mail in the White House's Oval Office in March.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普总统持有其签署的行政命令，呼吁限制 3 月份在白宫椭圆形办公室通过邮寄投票。

contentParagraphs.en: A federal judge has declined to temporarily block President Trump's executive order that calls for restricting voting by mail.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一名联邦法官拒绝暂时阻止特朗普总统要求限制邮寄投票的行政命令。

contentParagraphs.en: The ruling released Thursday by U.S. District Judge Carl Nichols, a Trump nominee based in Washington, D.C., leaves in place — at least for now — an executive order on voting that tests the limits of the president's power under the Constitution. A separate, 2025 executive order on voting was halted by courts.
contentParagraphs.zh: 华盛顿特区的特朗普提名人、美国地区法官卡尔·尼科尔斯周四发布的裁决保留了——至少目前如此——一项关于投票的行政命令，该命令测试了宪法规定的总统权力的限制。法院中止了一项单独的 2025 年投票行政命令。

contentParagraphs.en: The latest executive order, issued March 31, calls for the Department of Homeland Security to work with the Social Security Administration to create lists of adult U.S. citizens in each state, and to send those lists to state election officials. It also calls for the U.S. Postal Service — a federal agency that's independent of a president's administration — to come up with lists of eligible voters and to only deliver mail-in ballots to people on those lists.
contentParagraphs.zh: 3 月 31 日发布的最新行政命令要求国土安全部与社会保障管理局合作，创建每个州的成年美国公民名单，并将这些名单发送给州选举官员。它还要求美国邮政服务（一个独立于总统政府的联邦机构）制定合格选民名单，并只向名单上的人邮寄选票。

contentParagraphs.en: "The Court recognizes that the Postal Service may ultimately issue a final rule that directly affects Plaintiffs or their members, or that the Government may develop State Citizenship Lists that omit specific individuals due to particularized flaws. Plaintiffs may, of course, renew their motions if and when those future actions occur. Until then, however, Plaintiffs cannot show that preliminary injunctive relief is warranted," Nichols wrote about the decision not to block the order.
contentParagraphs.zh: “法院认识到，邮政服务最终可能会发布直接影响原告或其成员的最终规则，或者政府可能会制定国家公民名单，由于特定的缺陷而忽略特定个人。当然，如果未来发生这些行动，原告可以更新他们的动议。然而，在此之前，原告无法证明初步禁令救济是必要的，”尼科尔斯在谈到不阻止该命令的决定时写道。

contentParagraphs.en: Nichols' ruling comes as another federal judge is preparing to issue a ruling in the coming weeks for a similar set of lawsuits based in Boston.
contentParagraphs.zh: 尼科尔斯做出裁决之际，另一位联邦法官正准备在未来几周内对波士顿的一系列类似诉讼做出裁决。

contentParagraphs.en: Since Trump signed the order, it's been unclear whether and how it would actually affect mail-in voting, which has been taking place for state primaries in this year's midterm election. In early May, the administration said in a court filing that federal agencies were still deliberating how to carry out the order. Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche later told a Senate Appropriations subcommittee that the Justice Department is working with other agencies to "make sure" the order's goals are implemented.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自特朗普签署该命令以来，尚不清楚它是否以及如何真正影响今年中期选举中州初选的邮寄投票。 5月初，政府在一份法庭文件中表示，联邦机构仍在审议如何执行该命令。美国代理司法部长托德·布兰奇后来告诉参议院拨款小组委员会，司法部正在与其他机构合作，“确保”该命令的目标得到实施。

contentParagraphs.en: Democrats, voting rights groups and almost two dozen states, plus Washington, D.C., have filed five lawsuits challenging the order.
contentParagraphs.zh: 民主党、投票权团体和近二十几个州，加上华盛顿特区，已提起五起诉讼，质疑该命令。

contentParagraphs.en: They argue that Article I of the Constitution gives state legislatures and Congress — not the president — the power to set rules for federal elections. Their lawsuits also contend that Trump's order directs USPS to make rules about election mail that would overstep the mailing agency's authority.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他们认为，宪法第一条赋予州立法机关和国会（而不是总统）制定联邦选举规则的权力。他们的诉讼还称，特朗普的命令指示美国邮政局制定有关选举邮件的规则，这将超越邮寄机构的权力。

contentParagraphs.en: Trump, who himself voted by mail in Florida in March, has said he issued the order to stop illegal voting by noncitizens in federal elections, which reviews and research have found to be incredibly rare. While there are voters across the partisan divide who rely on mail-in voting, more registered Democrats than Republicans say they voted by mail in the last national election in 2024.
contentParagraphs.zh: 特朗普本人于三月份在佛罗里达州通过邮寄方式投票，他表示，他发布了禁止非公民在联邦选举中非法投票的命令，审查和研究发现这种情况极为罕见。尽管不同党派的选民都依赖邮寄投票，但表示在 2024 年上一次全国选举中通过邮寄投票的登记民主党人多于共和党人。

contentParagraphs.en: The new court ruling on Trump's order comes out of the three lawsuits filed in federal court in D.C. A decision on a similar request to block provisions of the order may come out of the two Massachusetts-based lawsuits as soon as early June.
contentParagraphs.zh: 法院对特朗普命令的新裁决是在华盛顿特区联邦法院提起的三起诉讼中作出的。马萨诸塞州的两起诉讼最早可能会在 6 月初针对阻止该命令条款的类似请求做出决定。

contentParagraphs.en: Edited by Benjamin Swasey
contentParagraphs.zh: 本杰明·斯瓦西编辑

articles.title: Israel intensifies attacks on southern Lebanon, killing at least 16 people
articles.summary: Southern Lebanon empties out under bombardment as Red Cross warns of worsening crisis.
articles.titleZh: 以色列加强对黎巴嫩南部的袭击 造成至少16人死亡
articles.summaryZh: 红十字会警告危机恶化，黎巴嫩南部空无一人。

contentParagraphs.en: Southern Lebanon empties out under bombardment as Red Cross warns of worsening crisis.At least 16 people have been killed and 58 wounded in Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon, according to Lebanese health authorities, as Israel intensifies its assault and issues mass displacement orders across the region.Lebanon’s state-run National News Agency reported on Thursday that six of the victims belonged to the same family. They were killed in an Israeli drone strike while trying to flee at dawn along the Adloun Highway, a key route linking Sidon and Tyre, it said.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3Why is Israel ramping up attacks in Lebanon despite a ceasefire?list 2 of 3What’s happening on Iran war day 90 as US launches new strikeslist 3 of 3Israeli attack on Gaza City kills at least 10, including four childrenend of listThe attack came as Israel expanded its bombardment across southern Lebanon, hitting residential areas, roads and civilian infrastructure.The Lebanese army said one of its soldiers was killed in an Israeli strike in the Nabatieh area, the latest in a string of attacks targeting military personnel. Other soldiers have also been killed in recent days in strikes across southern Lebanon and the western Beqaa Valley.Israel has also issued sweeping displacement orders covering large parts of the south, including Tyre and surrounding areas. Residents were told to evacuate immediately and move north of the Zahrani River, roughly 40km (25 miles) from the Lebanon-Israel border.The orders, shared on social media with marked buildings, have forced thousands of people to flee under threat of attack.‘Things are only going to get worse’In Tyre, Israeli air strikes hit a building and a cafe overnight, setting off fires and leaving emergency crews searching through debris for casualties.The Israeli military said it is targeting what it calls Hezbollah “infrastructure”, but Israeli strikes have repeatedly hit civilian neighbourhoods and populated areas.Al Jazeera’s Obaida Hitto, reporting from Tyre, said the attacks on the city were relentless.“Since midnight, there have been over a dozen strikes on the city of Tyre and its surrounding areas.”“Yesterday, people left the city over the course of the day. It seems like the city, for the most part, is pretty empty,” he added.The International Committee of the Red Cross has warned that Israel’s large-scale forced displacements and continued strikes on urban centres are placing civilians at severe risk and deepening a humanitarian crisis.“The fear is that things are only going to get worse,” Hitto said.Al Jazeera’s Zeina Khodr, reporting from Beirut, said a US-brokered ceasefire that took effect last month had failed to halt the violence.“It was just ink on paper. It remained very much an active warzone.”“But what we have seen over the past three or four days is some of the heaviest Israeli bombardment yet across southern Lebanon since this latest confrontation began in early March,” she added.The escalation came as United States-mediated talks between Israel and Lebanon are due to resume on Thursday, beginning with technical discussions before moving to broader negotiations in early June.However, Israel has significantly intensified its military operations in the past two days, casting doubt over the prospects for diplomacy.“It is unclear how these talks will proceed,” Hitto said.“People here are extremely frustrated and would like to see the Lebanese government take a stronger position at these talks, but that doesn’t look like it’s going to happen,” he added.
contentParagraphs.zh: 红十字会警告危机恶化，黎巴嫩南部空无一人。据黎巴嫩卫生当局称，以色列在黎巴嫩南部发动的袭击已造成至少 16 人死亡、58 人受伤，以色列加大了袭击力度，并在整个地区发布了大规模流离失所令。黎巴嫩国营国家通讯社周四报道称，其中 6 名受害者属于同一家庭。报道称，黎明时分，他们试图沿阿德隆高速公路（连接西顿和提尔的一条重要路线）逃离，但在一次以色列无人机袭击中丧生。推荐故事列表（共 3 项）列表 1（共 3 项）为什么以色列在停火后仍加大对黎巴嫩的袭击力度？列表 2（共 3 项）美国发动新的空袭，伊朗战争日 90 发生了什么列表 3 项（共 3 项）以色列对加沙城的袭击造成至少 10 人死亡，其中包括 4 名儿童end of listThe袭击发生之际，以色列扩大了对黎巴嫩南部的轰炸，袭击了居民区、道路和民用基础设施。黎巴嫩军队表示，一名士兵在以色列对纳巴提耶地区的袭击中丧生，这是针对军事人员的一系列袭击中的最新一起。最近几天，其他士兵也在黎巴嫩南部和贝卡谷地西部的袭击中丧生。以色列还发布了全面的流离失所令，覆盖南部大部分地区，包括提尔及周边地区。居民被告知立即撤离，转移到距黎巴嫩-以色列边境约 40 公里（25 英里）的扎赫拉尼河以北。这些命令在社交媒体上与标记的建筑物分享，迫使数千人在受到袭击威胁的情况下逃离。“情况只会变得更糟”。在提尔，以色列的空袭连夜袭击了一座建筑物和一家咖啡馆，引发火灾，让紧急救援人员在废墟中搜寻伤亡人员。以色列军方表示，他们的目标是所谓的目标。真主党的“基础设施”，但以色列的袭击一再袭击平民社区和人口稠密地区。半岛电视台记者奥贝达·希托从提尔报道称，对该市的袭击是无情的。“自午夜以来，提尔市及其周边地区发生了十几次袭击。”“昨天，人们在一天中离开了这座城市。看起来这座城市大部分都是空的，”他补充道。红十字会警告说，以色列的大规模强迫流离失所和对城市中心的持续袭击使平民面临严重危险，并加剧了人道主义危机。希托说：“令人担心的是，事情只会变得更糟。”半岛电视台记者泽纳·科德尔在贝鲁特报道称，上个月在美国斡旋下生效的停火协议未能阻止暴力活动。“这只是纸上谈兵。它仍然是一个非常活跃的战区。”她补充说，过去三四天内，以色列对黎巴嫩南部进行了自三月初最近一次对抗以来最猛烈的轰炸。事态升级之际，美国调解的以色列和黎巴嫩之间的谈判将于周四恢复，首先进行技术讨论，然后在六月初进行更广泛的谈判。然而，以色列在过去两天显着加强了军事行动，使人们对外交前景产生了怀疑。“目前尚不清楚这些谈判将如何进行，”Hitto “这里的人们非常沮丧，希望看到黎巴嫩政府在这些谈判中采取更强硬的立场，但这看起来不会发生，”他补充道。

articles.title: Man arrested after three injured in stabbing at Swiss train station
articles.summary: The suspect, a 31-year-old Swiss man, has been arrested, police say, as areas around the station are cordoned off.
articles.titleZh: 瑞士火车站刺伤三人受伤男子被捕
articles.summaryZh: 警方称，由于车站周围地区已被封锁，嫌疑人是一名 31 岁的瑞士男子，他已被逮捕。

articles.title: The West Bank’s Youth Unemployment Crisis
articles.summary: The West Bank's Youth Unemployment Crisis

contentParagraphs.en: The West Bank's Youth Unemployment CrisisNewsFeedThere are only enough jobs for about half the students who graduate university here in the occupied West Bank. But that isn’t stopping Palestinians from finishing school and celebrating. Al Jazeera’s Leila Warah spoke to the generation bearing the brunt of it.By Leila WarahPublished On 28 May 2026
contentParagraphs.zh: 约旦河西岸的青年失业危机新闻动态 在被占领的约旦河西岸，只有大约一半的大学毕业生能够找到工作。但这并不能阻止巴勒斯坦人完成学业并庆祝。半岛电视台的莱拉·瓦拉 (Leila Warah) 对首当其冲的一代人发表了讲话。 作者：莱拉·瓦拉 (Leila Warah) 发布于 2026 年 5 月 28 日

articles.title: Israel kills children in strikes on Gaza as fear taints Eid joy
articles.summary: As Muslims around the world celebrate Eid, Palestinians in Gaza are facing fear, grief, and deadly attacks.
articles.titleZh: 以色列在加沙空袭中杀害儿童，因为恐惧破坏了开斋节的欢乐
articles.summaryZh: 当世界各地的穆斯林庆祝开斋节时，加沙的巴勒斯坦人正面临恐惧、悲伤和致命的袭击。

contentParagraphs.en: Israel kills children in strikes on Gaza as fear taints Eid joyNewsFeedAs Muslims around the world celebrate Eid, Palestinians in Gaza are facing fear, grief, and deadly attacks.
contentParagraphs.zh: 以色列在加沙空袭中杀害儿童，恐惧玷污了开斋节的欢乐新闻动态当世界各地的穆斯林庆祝开斋节时，加沙的巴勒斯坦人正面临恐惧、悲伤和致命的袭击。

contentParagraphs.en: Overnight strikes in Gaza City killed children near one of the Strip’s few remaining playgrounds, leaving families terrified to leave their tents on what should have been a day of joy and peace.Published On 28 May 2026
contentParagraphs.zh: 加沙城的隔夜罢工导致加沙地带仅存的几个游乐场之一附近的儿童死亡，让许多家庭在本应是欢乐与和平的一天离开帐篷。发布于 2026 年 5 月 28 日

articles.title: Ireland football coach urges players to ‘win this war’ against Israel
articles.summary: Ireland are set to play Israel in home and away Nations League matches, but there has been pressure to boycott games.
articles.titleZh: 爱尔兰足球教练敦促球员“赢得这场针对以色列的战争”
articles.summaryZh: 爱尔兰将在国家联赛主客场对阵以色列，但抵制比赛的压力一直存在。

contentParagraphs.en: Ireland are set to play Israel in home and away Nations League matches, but there has been pressure to boycott games.Republic of Ireland manager Heimir Hallgrimsson has challenged his players to “win this war” against Israel when the teams meet in the Nations League amid renewed protests in the Irish parliament about the games.The Football Association of Ireland has been under pressure to boycott the two matches, including a home fixture in Dublin on October 4.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Neymar injury threatens Brazil forward’s World Cup as scans needed on calflist 2 of 4Messi’s GOAT statue in India to be removed over safety concernslist 3 of 4‘Why not us?’: Does the US really have a chance of lifting the World Cup?list 4 of 4FIFA subpoenaed by New York, New Jersey in World Cup ticket investigationend of listSeveral pro-Palestinian Irish politicians and football figures have urged the federation to withdraw from the fixtures over Israel’s conduct during its genocidal war on Gaza.The FAI passed a motion in November requesting that UEFA immediately suspend Israel from international competition, but received no support from European football’s governing body.There were new protests at the Dail – the Irish parliament – on Wednesday over the fixtures.In October, Icelandic boss Hallgrimsson was among those calling for Israel to be banned from international football competition.The 58-year-old, speaking ahead of a friendly game against Qatar in Dublin, said: “I’ve already expressed my opinion on this matter, you don’t need to repeat that one.“But from a footballing perspective – it’s an obstacle I don’t like, and it’s unfair for the players to be in this position and us to be in this position – but from a footballing perspective, I don’t like us to be the bad guys. We are not the bad guys here.”He added: “I think the best answer for us is just to win this game, to win this war against them [Israel]. That would be a perfect answer from my end.“Other decisions are not made by us, but all the players would like to represent the country and do well for them, no matter who is the opponent.”Israel played their World Cup qualifiers in Hungary and are set to use a neutral venue for their Nations League match against Ireland on September 27.Such has been the outcry in Ireland concerning the October 4 match in Dublin in particular, there have been suggestions it could be played elsewhere, with the FAI insisting in February it had “no choice” but to fulfil the two fixtures against Israel.Hallgrimsson, however, said: “It would obviously be a disadvantage from footballing reasons not to play at home. We’ve done really well at home.“It’s building to be a strong fortress for us, Aviva [Lansdowne Road], and to move it obviously will lower our chances, so I hope people can rally around the players and us playing instead of protesting or doing something that’s damaging.”
contentParagraphs.zh: 爱尔兰将在国家联赛主场和客场对阵以色列，但抵制比赛的压力一直存在。在爱尔兰议会再次对比赛提出抗议之际，爱尔兰共和国主帅海米尔·哈尔格里姆森向他的球员发起挑战，要求他的球员在国家联赛中对阵以色列时“赢得这场战争”。爱尔兰足协一直面临抵制这两场比赛的压力，其中包括 10 月 4 日在都柏林举行的主场比赛。推荐故事列表 4 项列表（共 4 项）内马尔受伤威胁巴西前锋的世界杯，因为小牛需要扫描 list 2 of 4 出于安全考虑，印度的梅西山羊雕像将被移除 list 3 of 4“为什么不是我们？”：美国真的有机会举起世界杯吗？ list 4 4 国际足联在世界杯门票调查中被纽约、新泽西传唤 名单结束 几位亲巴勒斯坦的爱尔兰政治家和足球人物敦促联合会退出比赛以色列在加沙种族灭绝战争期间的行为。FAI于11月通过了一项动议，要求欧足联立即暂停以色列参加国际比赛，但没有得到欧洲足球管理机构的支持。周三，爱尔兰议会因赛程引发了新的抗议。10月，冰岛主帅哈尔格里姆森是呼吁禁止以色列参加国际足球比赛的人之一。这位58岁的球员在都柏林与卡塔尔的友谊赛前发表讲话说： “我已经就这件事表达了我的看法，你不需要重复这一点。“但从足球的角度来看——这是我不喜欢的障碍，球员处于这个位置，我们处于这个位置是不公平的——但从足球的角度来看，我不喜欢我们成为坏人。我们不是这里的坏人。”他补充道：“我认为对我们来说最好的答案就是赢得这场比赛，赢得这场针对他们（以色列）的战争。这将是我最后一个完美的答案。“其他决定不是由我们做出的，但所有球员都愿意代表国家并为他们做好准备，无论对手是谁。”以色列在匈牙利进行了世界杯预选赛，并将在 9 月 27 日对阵爱尔兰的国家联赛中使用中立场地。爱尔兰对 10 月 4 日在都柏林举行的比赛表示强烈抗议，有人建议比赛可以在其他地方进行，而 FAI 坚持认为比赛可以在其他地方进行二月份，它“别无选择”，只能完成对阵以色列的两场比赛。然而，霍尔格里姆森表示：“出于足球原因，不在主场比赛显然是不利的。我们在主场做得非常好。“它正在成为我们英杰华（兰斯当路）的坚固堡垒，而移动它显然会降低我们的机会，所以我希望人们能够团结在球员和我们比赛周围，而不是抗议或做一些有破坏性的事情。

articles.title: Argentina’s ‘El Loco’ faces Uruguay mutiny ahead of World Cup
articles.summary: Marcelo Bielsa's demanding coaching style faces internal threat in Uruguay camp ahead of World Cup as results slide.
articles.titleZh: 阿根廷“疯子”在世界杯前面临乌拉圭叛变
articles.summaryZh: 随着成绩下滑，马塞洛·贝尔萨苛刻的执教风格在世界杯前的乌拉圭阵营中面临着内部威胁。

contentParagraphs.en: Marcelo Bielsa is widely revered as a pioneer of coaching, but his unconventional methods risk ruining Uruguay’s World Cup chances before the tournament has even begun, with rumours of dressing room unrest.Nicknamed “El Loco”, which means madman, the 70-year-old’s bold, attacking approach has proved an inspiration to a younger generation of coaches, including Pep Guardiola and USA boss Mauricio Pochettino.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4Scotland fans book 20 school buses for games at ‘inaccessible’ US World Cuplist 2 of 4Messi’s GOAT statue in India to be removed over safety concernslist 3 of 4How Senegal became genuine World Cup 2026 contenders – at a costlist 4 of 4World Cup broadcast hopes boosted in India as Zee Entertainment in talksend of listYet, his famously demanding standards have often caused friction during a nomadic coaching career, and his stint in Uruguay has been no exception.The Argentinian’s arrival initially generated huge excitement, which was fuelled by landmark victories over Brazil and Argentina in qualifying.But they needed that flying start just to make it through after winning just three of their final 12 qualifiers.The tipping point for many in the squad came at the Copa America in 2024.Uruguay finished a creditable third, eliminating Brazil along the way, but Bielsa’s intensity during the monthlong tournament did not endear him to his players.Luis Suarez hit out at Bielsa’s methods after retiring from international football months later, claiming he had reduced former Liverpool striker Darwin Nunez to tears at half-time of a 2-0 win over Argentina, such was the force of his criticism.Bielsa accepted that after the former Barcelona striker’s backlash, his “authority was affected” with the rest of the dressing room.Results have also regressed, with Bielsa stating he was “ashamed” by a 5-1 friendly defeat by the USA in November.Now, as his third World Cup with a third different nation approaches, the question is whether Bielsa can win back the faith of his players for a country so used to punching above its weight on the world stage.And there are doubts as to how his high-energy style will fare in the gruelling conditions of Miami and Guadalajara, where Uruguay will face Saudi Arabia, Cape Verde and Spain in Group F.Pochettino hails Bielsa as a ‘genius’Bielsa made his name winning three league titles in Argentina with Newell’s Old Boys, where the stadium now bears his name, and Velez Sarsfield.“For me, he’s a person I will always admire,” said Pochettino, whom Bielsa recruited for Newell’s as a 13-year-old.“He’s a genius. A person with charisma and a personality very different from us, normal coaches, and that’s what makes him special.”Bielsa’s sides in Bilbao with Athletic Club, where he reached the Europa League and Copa del Rey finals in 2011-12, and Marseille, where he led Ligue 1 at the halfway stage in 2014-15, were also admired but ended up empty-handed as their energy ran out.In Leeds, murals still bear Bielsa’s face, four years on from his departure, after he led a sleeping giant of English football back to the Premier League for the first time in 16 years in 2020.His time there ended in familiar fashion with an exhausted squad that was relegated to the second tier the season after he departed.Yet, the esteem with which he is held for his daring tactical approach endures.“To be loved is this biggest title, bigger than the Champions League or Premier League or whatever,” said Guardiola, who went to visit Bielsa in Argentina before setting out as a coach at Barcelona.“To be loved is the most important thing, and I think Marcelo has that more than any other manager in the world.”Bielsa, who oversaw Argentina’s group-stage elimination in 2002 and Chile’s round-of-16 loss to hosts Brazil in 2014, has already hinted ‌that he may not remain as manager ⁠of Uruguay beyond ⁠July, saying his job with the team ends with the World Cup.“Our job ends with the World Cup,” ⁠Bielsa said at an event organised by the Uruguayan Football Association last Friday.Although he did not elaborate on his remarks, local media reported ⁠that the Argentinian will not continue once his current contract expires at the end of the June 11-July 19 tournament.“It is a miracle in any ‌professional’s sporting career to take part in the World Cup,” he said. “I will be forever grateful to Uruguay for allowing me to enjoy a competition like the World Cup.”
contentParagraphs.zh: 马塞洛·贝尔萨被广泛尊为教练先驱，但他的非传统方法有可能在比赛开始前就毁掉乌拉圭世界杯的机会，甚至有更衣室骚乱的传言。这位70岁老人绰号“El Loco”，意思是疯子，他大胆的进攻方式给包括瓜迪奥拉和美国队主教练毛里西奥·波切蒂诺在内的年轻一代教练带来了灵感。 4 项目列表 4 中的 1 苏格兰球迷预订了 20 辆校车参加“难以到达”的美国世界杯 列表 4 中的 2 梅西在印度的山羊雕像因安全问题将被拆除列表 4 中的 3 塞内加尔如何成为 2026 年世界杯真正的竞争者 - 成本高昂 列表 4 中的 4 世界杯转播希望在印度增强，因为 Zee 娱乐在列表的谈话中 然而，他著名的苛刻标准经常在游牧期间引起摩擦教练生涯，他在乌拉圭的任期也不例外。阿根廷人的到来最初引起了巨大的兴奋，而在预选赛中对巴西和阿根廷取得的里程碑式的胜利更是推动了这种兴奋。但他们需要一个良好的开局，才能在最后 12 场预选赛中只赢得三场后成功晋级。对队中许多人来说，转折点出现在 2024 年的美洲杯上。乌拉圭队获得了值得称赞的第三名，一路淘汰了巴西队，但贝尔萨的球队长达一个月的比赛期间的强度并没有让他受到球员们的喜爱。几个月后从国际足坛退役后，路易斯·苏亚雷斯猛烈抨击了贝尔萨的方法，声称他在2-0战胜阿根廷的比赛中半场让前利物浦前锋达尔文·努涅斯落泪，这就是他批评的力量。贝尔萨承认，在这位前巴塞罗那前锋的强烈反对之后，他与更衣室其他人的“权威受到了影响”。结果也有所倒退，贝尔萨表示，他对11月友谊赛以5比1输给美国队感到“羞愧”。现在，随着他第三次与第三个不同的国家队参加世界杯，问题是贝尔萨是否能赢回球员们对这个习惯于在世界舞台上超常发挥的国家的信心。而且人们怀疑他的高能量风格将如何在迈阿密和瓜达拉哈拉的艰苦条件下发挥作用，乌拉圭将在小组中面对沙特阿拉伯、佛得角和西班牙波切蒂诺称赞贝尔萨是“天才”。贝尔萨在阿根廷随纽维尔老男孩队和贝莱斯萨斯菲尔德赢得了三个联赛冠军，现在球场以他的名字命名。“对我来说，他是一个我永远钦佩的人，”贝尔萨在 13 岁时为纽维尔队招募的波切蒂诺说道。贝尔萨在毕尔巴鄂竞技俱乐部的球队，他在 2011-12 赛季打进了欧罗巴联赛和国王杯决赛，而在马赛，他在 2014-15 赛季的半程阶段带领法甲球队也受到了赞赏，但最终因为能量耗尽而空手而归。在利兹，壁画上仍然刻着贝尔萨的脸，四年过去了2020年，他带领这支沉睡的英格兰足球巨人时隔16年首次重返英超。他在英超的时光以一种熟悉的方式结束，球队在他离开后的那个赛季被降级到了第二级别联赛。然而，他因大胆的战术方法而受到的尊重依然存在。“被爱是这个最大的冠军，比冠军联赛或英超联赛或其他任何东西都重要，”前往参观的瓜迪奥拉说道。贝尔萨在前往巴塞罗那担任教练之前在阿根廷。“被爱是最重要的事情，我认为马塞洛比世界上任何其他教练都更能做到这一点。”贝尔萨曾在 2002 年带领阿根廷队小组赛被淘汰，并在 2014 年带领智利队在 16 强中输给东道主巴西队，他已经暗示他可能不会在 7 月份之后继续担任乌拉圭队主教练，并表示他在球队的工作将在上周五，贝尔萨在乌拉圭足协组织的一次活动中表示：“我们的工作随着世界杯而结束。”尽管他没有详细说明自己的言论，但当地媒体报道称，一旦他目前的合同在 6 月 11 日至 7 月 19 日世界杯结束后到期，阿根廷人将不会继续继续踢球。“参加世界杯对于任何职业球员的体育生涯来说都是一个奇迹，”他说。 “我将永远感激乌拉圭让我享受世界杯这样的比赛。”

articles.title: US justice department launches criminal investigation into Trump accuser E Jean Carroll, reports say
articles.summary: The case centres on whether the former columnist lied about the funding she received for her civil lawsuit against the US president.
articles.titleZh: 报道称，美国司法部对特朗普指控者让·卡罗尔展开刑事调查
articles.summaryZh: 该案的重点是这位前专栏作家是否对她针对美国总统的民事诉讼所获得的资金撒了谎。

articles.title: An Ohio pastor-turned-lawmaker backs a Charlie Kirk American Heritage Act for schools
articles.summary: The bill says it would permit the teaching of the positive impact of Judeo-Christian values in U.S. history. Opponents say it offers a skewed view of history.
articles.titleZh: 俄亥俄州牧师出身的立法者支持《查理·柯克美国学校遗产法案》
articles.summaryZh: 该法案表示，它将允许教授犹太基督教价值观对美国历史的积极影响。反对者表示，它提供了一种扭曲的历史观。

contentParagraphs.en: Republican state Rep. Gary Click, from northern Ohio, is a Baptist pastor and three-term lawmaker. Here, he poses for a portrait in his office in Columbus, Ohio, in February.
contentParagraphs.zh: 来自俄亥俄州北部的共和党州众议员加里·克里克 (Gary Click) 是一名浸信会牧师和三届立法者。这张照片是二月，他在俄亥俄州哥伦布市的办公室里摆姿势拍照。

contentParagraphs.en: Andrew Spear for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安德鲁·斯皮尔

contentParagraphs.en: Andrew Spear for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安德鲁·斯皮尔

contentParagraphs.en: Ohio state Rep. Gary Click recalls the comfort he felt going to church as a child and when he declared his faith before a congregation at the age of 12.
contentParagraphs.zh: 俄亥俄州众议员加里·克里克 (Gary Click) 回忆起他小时候去教堂以及 12 岁时在会众面前宣示信仰时所感受到的安慰。

contentParagraphs.en: "I went down and I just told the Lord, I said, if you want me to be a pastor, I'll be a pastor," Click recounted in an interview.
contentParagraphs.zh: “我下去告诉主，我说，如果你想让我当牧师，我就当牧师，”克里克在接受采访时回忆道。

contentParagraphs.en: He did become a Baptist pastor — and later, a lawmaker. Click, 60, is a three-term Ohio legislator. God created three institutions, he says: the family, the home and the government.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他确实成为了一名浸信会牧师，后来又成为了一名立法者。 60 岁的克里克是一位连任三届的俄亥俄州议员。他说，上帝创造了三个机构：家庭、家庭和政府。

contentParagraphs.en: "As good stewards, we should be involved in all of those, to one extent or the other," Click says.
contentParagraphs.zh: “作为优秀的管理者，我们应该在某种程度上参与所有这些活动，”克里克说。

contentParagraphs.en: He's the architect of the state's ban on gender-affirming surgeries and hormone treatments for minors. He's backed a range of bills, from less-controversial ones like requiring schools to allow excused absences for religious reasons to hotly debated ones, like restricting abortion and requiring K-12 schools to let students leave during the day for religious study.
contentParagraphs.zh: 他是该州禁止对未成年人进行性别确认手术和激素治疗的创始人。他支持一系列法案，从争议较小的法案（例如要求学校允许因宗教原因缺勤）到备受争议的法案（例如限制堕胎和要求 K-12 学校允许学生在白天离开学校进行宗教学习）。

contentParagraphs.en: Artwork depicting the late conservative activist Charlie Kirk is seen in the office of Ohio state Rep. Gary Click.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在俄亥俄州众议员加里·克里克的办公室里可以看到描绘已故保守派活动家查理·柯克的艺术品。

contentParagraphs.en: Andrew Spear for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安德鲁·斯皮尔

contentParagraphs.en: Andrew Spear for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的安德鲁·斯皮尔

contentParagraphs.en: But Click says he's not legislating his own religion, because you don't have to be a Christian to agree with what he introduces and advances.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但克里克表示，他并没有为自己的宗教立法，因为你不必是基督徒也能同意他所介绍和推进的内容。

contentParagraphs.en: A bill named for Charlie Kirk about teaching religion's impact on America
contentParagraphs.zh: 以查理·柯克 (Charlie Kirk) 命名的一项关于教授宗教对美国影响的法案

contentParagraphs.en: "The Bible says 'Thou shalt not kill.' Now, am I legislating the Bible if I support laws against murder? No, I'm not," he says. "Says 'Thou shalt not steal.' If we have laws against theft, and actually, I have a burglary bill right now, am I legislating my religion?"
contentParagraphs.zh: “圣经说‘不可杀人。’”现在，如果我支持禁止谋杀的法律，我是否在为圣经立法？不，我不是，”他说。 “说‘不可偷盗。’如果我们有反盗窃的法律，事实上，我现在有一项防盗法案，我是否在立法我的宗教？”

contentParagraphs.en: Since last year, Click's been working on passing the Charlie Kirk American Heritage Act. It has passed the state House and is in the Senate.
contentParagraphs.zh: 自去年以来，克里克一直致力于通过《查理·柯克美国遗产法案》。该法案已在州众议院获得通过，并进入参议院。

contentParagraphs.en: The bill says it would permit the teaching of the positive impact of "Judeo-Christian" values in U.S. history. It lists two dozen examples, from appeals to divine power in the Declaration of Independence and the religious backgrounds of the signers, to the impact of evangelical Billy Graham.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该法案表示，它将允许教授“犹太基督教”价值观对美国历史的积极影响。它列出了两打例子，从《独立宣言》中对神圣力量的呼吁和签署者的宗教背景，到福音派比利·格雷厄姆的影响。

contentParagraphs.en: A couple of other states have similar bills, though they're not named after the slain conservative activist and occasional confidante of President Trump. Click says he hopes his will be a model.
contentParagraphs.zh: 其他几个州也有类似的法案，尽管它们不是以被杀的保守派活动家和特朗普总统偶尔的红颜知己的名字命名的。 Click 说他希望自己能成为一名模特。

contentParagraphs.en: Ohio state Rep. Mark Johnson prays in March during "Prayer at the Statehouse," a quarterly event hosted by the Center for Christian Virtue to pray with lawmakers in the state Capitol in Columbus, Ohio.
contentParagraphs.zh: 俄亥俄州众议员马克·约翰逊三月份在“州议会祈祷”期间祈祷，这是由基督教美德中心主办的季度活动，与俄亥俄州哥伦布州议会大厦的立法者一起祈祷。

contentParagraphs.en: Maddie McGarvey for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的麦迪·麦加维

contentParagraphs.en: Maddie McGarvey for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的麦迪·麦加维

contentParagraphs.en: Click named the bill after Kirk, whose rhetoric offended some but resonated with others, because he sees a connection.
contentParagraphs.zh: 克里克以柯克的名字命名了该法案，柯克的言论冒犯了一些人，但也引起了其他人的共鸣，因为他看到了其中的联系。

contentParagraphs.en: "One of the reasons that people hated Charlie, I think, is because he was advancing Christian principles. The Christian history of our nation. And people didn't like that. They hated that," he says. "They rejected that. And I think that's what took his life. And so I think people need a better education."
contentParagraphs.zh: “我认为，人们讨厌查理的原因之一是因为他正在推进基督教原则。我们国家的基督教历史。人们不喜欢这一点。他们讨厌这一点，”他说。 “他们拒绝了。我认为这就是他的生命。所以我认为人们需要更好的教育。”

contentParagraphs.en: Opponents say the bill would invite a skewed version of history
contentParagraphs.zh: 反对者表示，该法案将带来扭曲的历史版本

contentParagraphs.en: Opponents of the bill say it's unnecessary — or worse.
contentParagraphs.zh: 该法案的反对者表示，这是不必要的，甚至更糟。

contentParagraphs.en: "I have never heard of a single teacher in Ohio that says they're afraid to teach any of the content that's in this bill," says Ohio Council for the Social Studies President Sarah Kaka.
contentParagraphs.zh: 俄亥俄州社会研究委员会主席萨拉·卡卡 (Sarah Kaka) 表示：“我从未听说过俄亥俄州有哪个老师表示他们害怕教授该法案中的任何内容。”

contentParagraphs.en: She worries anytime the legislature is dictating the direction of educational content, she says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 她说，每当立法机关决定教育内容的方向时，她都会感到担忧。

contentParagraphs.en: "It is such a skewed perspective on history, right?" she says. "It's not balanced by any means, and our organization as a whole, we are very much proponents of historical inquiry, right? Teaching students not what to think, but how to think."
contentParagraphs.zh: “这是一种扭曲的历史观，对吗？”她说。 “无论如何，这都是不平衡的，我们的组织作为一个整体，我们非常支持历史探究，对吧？教学生的不是思考什么，而是如何思考。”

contentParagraphs.en: Groups backing laws that center on conservative views of Christianity go back generations, including the Moral Majority of the 1980s, and have won court backing in recent years with the elimination of the federal right to abortion and the weakening of bans on school prayer.
contentParagraphs.zh: 支持以基督教保守观点为中心的法律的团体可以追溯到几代人之前，其中包括 20 世纪 80 年代的道德多数派，并且近年来随着联邦堕胎权的取消和学校祈祷禁令的削弱而赢得了法院的支持。

contentParagraphs.en: People participate in "Prayer at the Statehouse" in March.
contentParagraphs.zh: 三月，人们参加“州议会祈祷”。

contentParagraphs.en: Maddie McGarvey for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的麦迪·麦加维

contentParagraphs.en: Maddie McGarvey for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的麦迪·麦加维

contentParagraphs.en: Indiana University Indianapolis professor Andrew Whitehead is among those who describe the movement as Christian Nationalism.
contentParagraphs.zh: 印第安纳大学印第安纳波利斯分校教授安德鲁怀特海德是将这场运动描述为基督教民族主义的人之一。

contentParagraphs.en: "[It's] the desire to fuse together a very particular expression of Christianity with American civic life, and then having the government at all levels, defend and preserve that connection and fusion," Whitehead says. He adds that this specific view sees an America primarily for white Protestant men and leans toward authoritarianism.
contentParagraphs.zh: “[这是]将基督教的一种非常特殊的表达方式与美国公民生活融合在一起的愿望，然后让各级政府捍卫和维护这种联系和融合，”怀特海德说。他补充说，这种特定观点认为美国主要是白人新教徒的国家，并且倾向于威权主义。

contentParagraphs.en: Click rejects the term. "That is a dog whistle," he says. "That's a crisis language in order to scare people and take them back to this idea of you're forcing your religion on us."
contentParagraphs.zh: 单击拒绝该术语。 “那是狗哨，”他说。 “这是一种危机语言，目的是吓唬人们，让他们回想起你把你的宗教信仰强加给我们的想法。”

contentParagraphs.en: Click is a member of the National Association of Christian Lawmakers, which shares model legislation against abortion access and transgender rights. He says the group is interested in the Charlie Kirk bill. Their website also includes proposals to ban same-sex marriage and to tie currency to gold and silver.
contentParagraphs.zh: Click 是全国基督教立法者协会的成员，该协会分享反对堕胎和变性人权利的示范立法。他说该组织对查理柯克法案感兴趣。他们的网站还包括禁止同性婚姻以及将货币与黄金和白银挂钩的提案。

contentParagraphs.en: In Ohio, legislation infused with religion has gained noticeable traction. The decades-old Center for Christian Virtue has notched "lots" of recent wins, President Aaron Baer says.
contentParagraphs.zh: 在俄亥俄州，充满宗教色彩的立法获得了显着的关注。具有数十年历史的基督教美德中心最近取得了“很多”胜利，主席亚伦·贝尔说。

contentParagraphs.en: Aaron Baer, president of the Center for Christian Virtue, speaks during "Prayer at the Statehouse" in March.
contentParagraphs.zh: 基督教美德中心主席亚伦·贝尔 (Aaron Baer) 在三月份的“州议会祈祷”期间发表讲话。

contentParagraphs.en: Maddie McGarvey for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的麦迪·麦加维

contentParagraphs.en: Maddie McGarvey for NPR
contentParagraphs.zh: NPR 的麦迪·麦加维

contentParagraphs.en: "It's never us," Baer says. "We don't have any votes, right? It's the lawmakers, but we worked with lawmakers on all of these issues."
contentParagraphs.zh: “从来都不是我们，”贝尔说。 “我们没有任何选票，对吧？是立法者，但我们在所有这些问题上与立法者合作。”

contentParagraphs.en: A few years ago, CCV bought a four-story building across the street from the Capitol.
contentParagraphs.zh: 几年前，CCV 购买了国会大厦街对面的一栋四层建筑。

contentParagraphs.en: Under Baer, CCV looks at legislation across the board, branching out from standard religious right issues — against abortion, obscenity, gambling — into lobbying on a range of
contentParagraphs.zh: 在贝尔的领导下，CCV 着眼于全面立法，从标准的宗教权利问题（反对堕胎、淫秽、赌博）扩展到游说一系列问题

articles.title: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
articles.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.
articles.titleZh: 伊朗称在美国发动新一轮袭击后，其目标是美国基地
articles.summaryZh: 敌对行动发生在美国和伊朗之间脆弱的停火期间，并且为结束三个月的战争而进行了旷日持久的谈判。

articles.title: Sixteen pupils killed in Kenya school fire
articles.summary: Dozens of people were also injured in the fire, the cause of which is not yet known.
articles.titleZh: 肯尼亚学校火灾 16 名学生丧生
articles.summaryZh: 火灾还造成数十人受伤，目前起火原因尚不清楚。
